opening_hooks: 25 million developers are asking the same terrifying question right now.
Devin, Cursor, Copilot: three products, one existential question for engineers.
Everyone's arguing about AI and coding. Almost nobody has the data.
Tech executives keep making bold claims. Here's what the numbers actually say.
Your job title might survive. Your job description almost certainly won't.
curiosity_hooks: We benchmarked AI coding tools against real engineers. Results surprised us.
The 'AI will replace engineers' debate is missing one critical data point.
What Stack Overflow's survey data reveals about your actual replacement risk.
Why the loudest AI predictions are usually made by people selling something.
The skill that determines if AI helps or replaces you isn't coding.
emotional_hooks: You didn't spend years learning to code just to be automated away.
The anxiety keeping engineers up at night deserves real answers, not hype.
Nobody warned you your career could change this fast. We're fixing that.
Your livelihood isn't a headline. It deserves evidence, not fear-mongering.
Feeling replaceable is exhausting. Clarity is the antidote to that fear.
authority_hooks: We built the Gartner of AI-in-software-engineering. Here's what it shows.
Our proprietary benchmark data cuts through the vendor hype and doom talk.
25M developers, one flagship report: the definitive State of AI in Engineering.
We don't sell AI tools. That's exactly why our data is trustworthy.
Editorial neutrality isn't our tagline—it's our entire competitive advantage.
contrarian_hooks: AI won't replace engineers. It will replace engineers who refuse to adapt.
The 'AI takes all coding jobs' narrative is mostly marketing, not reality.
Everyone's afraid of Devin. Almost nobody's read the actual benchmark data.
The doomers and the hypesters are both wrong—and both profiting from you.
Your job isn't disappearing. Your job description already has, quietly.
short_form_hooks: AI won't replace you. An engineer using AI will.
25M developers. One question. Zero honest answers—until now.
The data on AI coding tools nobody wants you to see.
Stop reading hot takes. Start reading benchmark data.
Your career fear, backed by actual evidence.
long_form_hooks: In a $150B market growing 30% annually, here's what actually happens to engineering careers when AI tools mature.
We surveyed the AI coding tool landscape—Devin, Cursor, Copilot—so you don't have to guess your career trajectory.
Between tech-exec hype and developer doom-scrolling lies the real story: proprietary data on AI's true impact on engineering jobs.
This is the flagship report 25 million anxious developers have been waiting for, built on evidence instead of executive soundbites.
Forget the polarized debate—here's a data-driven breakdown of which engineering skills AI augments, and which it actually replaces.
recommendations: Lead with data-specific hooks (benchmarks, survey numbers, report findings) to reinforce editorial authority positioning.
Use contrarian hooks sparingly on LinkedIn/Twitter to spark debate while maintaining neutral, evidence-based brand tone.
Pair emotional hooks with immediate authority follow-up (e.g., 'here's the data') to avoid appearing fear-mongering.
Test short-form hooks on Twitter/X and long-form hooks in LinkedIn articles or newsletter intros for channel fit.
Reserve strongest curiosity hooks for flagship report launch and gated content to drive email signups.
summary: These hooks position the brand as the neutral, data-driven authority on AI's impact on software engineering careers, balancing developer anxiety with evidence-based clarity across channels and content formats.
